Title: Matrix Concepts advances Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 with industrial land sale
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd is strengthening the industrial ecosystem within MVV City through the sale of industrial land to Hong Kong-listed Golog Holdings Ltd, as the township positions itself as an integrated industrial and logistics hub under Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 (MVV 2.0).
The collaboration reflects the property developer's strategy to attract industrial and logistics players into MVV City as part of its long-term vision to develop a future-ready and commercially competitive industrial ecosystem.
Golog's entry is expected to introduce cold chain infrastructure capabilities into the township, supporting rising demand for temperature-controlled storage, warehousing and distribution solutions across sectors including food processing, pharmaceuticals, e-commerce, logistics and import-export trade.
Matrix Concepts said the integration of cold chain infrastructure would strengthen MVV TechValley's competitiveness as regional supply chains evolve and businesses increasingly seek resilient and connected industrial destinations in Southeast Asia.
MVV City, jointly developed by Matrix Concepts and the Negeri Sembilan state government through NS Corporation, is the first integrated township within the MVV 2.0 corridor.
Spanning 964 hectares (ha), the development comprises 405ha of industrial land, over 15,000 homes and 70.4ha of commercial and institutional space, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM15 billion over the next 12 years.
The company said the addition of Golog's cold chain capabilities marks another step towards creating a more complete industrial ecosystem that integrates infrastructure, logistics readiness, business support facilities and connectivity to support long-term industrial growth.
Strategically located within the Seremban-MVV growth corridor, MVV City offers connectivity through major highways, proximity to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) and access to Port Klang, making it attractive for manufacturing, logistics and export-oriented industries.
Matrix Concepts said Golog's establishment in MVV City reflects growing investor confidence in the township's long-term industrial potential amid shifting regional supply chains and increasing Asean trade flows.
Further to an earlier memorandum of understanding signed by Golog for the development of 250 hectares of industrial land, the project will be rolled out in three phases with an estimated GDV of RM8 billion.
The development also aligns with regional logistics initiatives under the China-Malaysia Air Silk Road Dual Hub Industrial Park Cooperation Project.
Backed by Golog's strategic cooperation with China Henan Aviation Group Co., Ltd and Sichuan Huashi Group Corporation Ltd, the collaboration is expected to enhance regional logistics capabilities and strengthen cross-border supply chain connectivity.
Matrix Concepts said the partnership supports its goal of creating a commercially compelling industrial destination that offers long-term value for industrial buyers, investors and ecosystem partners operating within MVV City.
The company added that integrating cold chain infrastructure is expected to improve supply chain reliability, logistics efficiency and operational capabilities for businesses requiring temperature-sensitive handling and distribution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral Obstructed road near Titiwangsa fire station raises alarm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A viral post showing hawker stalls blocking roads near the Titiwangsa Fire and Rescue Station has sparked concerns over potential delays in emergency response.
The post, uploaded by the UpdateInfo page on X, drew criticism from netizens who questioned how fire engines would respond quickly during emergencies if the route remained congested.
The caption said complaints had already been lodged with the authorities, but enforcement action had yet to be taken.
"The fire station is way too close. If there's a fire, how are fire trucks supposed to get through if tables are blocking the road?" it said.
Images and videos shared online showed food stalls operating along the roadside, with tables set up in yellow parking bays meant for public use.
Several cars and motorcycles were also seen parked haphazardly in the area, further narrowing the road and potentially delaying emergency response times.
Netizens also pointed to signage by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) stating "No Hawking In Tow-Away Zone", but claimed hawkers continued operating there despite the warning.
The post also alleged that some hawkers were operating without valid licences in the restricted area.
The New Straits Times has reached out to DBKL for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: After Selangor ruler's decree, Ronnie Liu revisits Rukun Negara
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu took heed of Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah's decree when he visited Dataran Selangor to "deeply reflect" and read the Rukun Negara once again.
The former DAP leader said he was "deeply moved" during the visit as he came to better understand the history and spirit behind the national pillars.
He also urged political leaders from both the government and the opposition to do the same and seriously reflect on whether their past political strategies, rhetoric and actions truly upheld the spirit of the Federal Constitution, respected the rule of law and contributed to national unity and stability.
"Because what truly stabilises a nation is civilisation, institutions, rule of law, mutual respect, and social unity," he said in a post on his Facebook page today.
On his revisit to the Rukun Negara, Liu said many people in the past only memorised the principles, but standing there today and truly understanding the history and spirit behind it.
"It made me realise that what our national forefathers left behind was not merely a few slogans, but a set of national wisdom forged through one of the country's darkest moments," he said.
He said the true value of the Rukun Negara lies not only in its five principles but also in the spirit behind them.
"It is to ensure that people of different races, religions and cultural backgrounds can continue to live together peacefully, respect one another and build the future together.
"Belief in God, Loyalty to King and Country, Supremacy of the Constitution, Rule of Law, and Good Behaviour and Morality — these few principles encapsulate almost all the core values required for the long-term stability of a multiracial nation.
"Especially in today's era, where politics is becoming increasingly emotional and society is increasingly vulnerable to extreme polarisation, one begins to truly appreciate the depth, wisdom, and lasting value of the Rukun Negara," he said.
He also sincerely thanked the national forefathers who established the Rukun Negara.
"Only today are we beginning to fully realise that what they left behind was not merely words, but an important guiding direction for the continued stability and progress of this nation," he said.
It was reported yesterday that Sultan of Selangor told a DAP assemblyman and a former DAP
leader to read the Rukun Negara every week
to understand its contents and become courteous citizens who respect the royal institution.
The Ruler had said that all assemblymen in the state should go to Dataran Selangor twice a month to read the Rukun Negara inscribed on a plaque at the square.
"This is especially the case for Seri Kembangan assemblyman, YB Wong Siew Ki of the DAP and DAP's former state exco, Ronnie Liu," the Sultan of Selangor had said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine Supreme Court won't stop ICC arrest of senator
Article:
MANILA: The Philippines Supreme Court refused Wednesday to grant interim relief from arrest to a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity, a court statement said.
The interim ruling clears the way for the potential arrest of Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, the fugitive chief enforcer of ex-president Rodrigo Duterte's deadly war on drugs that claimed thousands of lives.
"The Supreme Court, in a vote of 9-5-1, denied the prayer for a temporary restraining order and/or status quo ante order filed by Senator Ronald "Bato" M. Dela Rosa," the court statement said.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt hits 50pct of spending cut target, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government's review of operating expenditure across ministries has achieved nearly 50 per cent of its target, says government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Fahmi said Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim informed the cabinet that the expenditure rationalisation exercise had begun yielding positive results without disrupting service delivery or ministry programmes.
He said priority would continue to be given to strengthening frontline services, particularly in health, security and education.
"The prime minister said that the estimated target has now reached about 50 per cent," he said at his weekly post-cabinet media briefing today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's trade surges 28.6pct to record RM336.7bil in April
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade expanded 28.6 per cent year-on-year in April 2026, the fastest growth since September 2022, to RM336.73 billion, driven by strong export demand across key markets and products.
In a statement, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister (MITI) said the country's trade performance remained resilient, with trade, exports and imports all reaching record monthly highs, despite rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia, higher logistics costs, supply chain disruptions and commodity price volatility.
Exports rose 36.9 per cent to a record RM182.74 billion, marking the 10th consecutive month of expansion and surpassing the previous high of RM152.77 billion recorded in December 2023 by RM30 billion.
Imports increased 20 per cent to RM153.99 billion, resulting in a trade surplus of RM28.75 billion, extending Malaysia's streak of trade surplus to 72 consecutive months since May 2020.
The strong export performance was mainly supported by double-digit growth in major export products, led by electrical and electronic (E&E) products, which climbed RM28 billion to a new record high amid robust demand from the artificial intelligence (AI) and automotive electronics segments.
Exports of petroleum products, manufacturers of metal, optical and scientific equipment as well as machinery, equipment and parts also recorded significant increases.
Exports of optical and scientific equipment as well as machinery, equipment and parts reached their highest monthly values on record.
Exports to all major trading partners, including Asean, China, the United States (US), Taiwan and the European Union (EU), posted strong double-digit growth, while exports to Asean, Taiwan and the EU hit record highs during the month.
Meanwhile, Malaysia's free trade agreements (FTAs) continued to support market access diversification, with exports to FTA partners such as Hong Kong, South Korea, Australia, India and Turkiye reaching new highs on sustained demand across various products.
For the January to April 2026 period, total trade surpassed RM1 trillion within four months, one month earlier compared with the previous year.
Trade grew 15.3 per cent to RM1.127 trillion, supported by exports, which rose 19 per cent to RM609.31 billion, while imports expanded 11.1 per cent to RM517.4 billion.
The trade surplus doubled year on year to RM91.92 billion, with trade, exports, imports and trade surplus all recording their highest levels for the period.
According to the Department of Statistics Malaysia, Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) expanded 5.4 per cent year on year in the first quarter of 2026, compared with 4.4 per cent growth in the corresponding quarter last year.
Meanwhile, the International Monetary Fund revised upward Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast for 2026 to 4.7 per cent in its April 2026 World Economic Outlook, reflecting confidence in the country's macroeconomic fundamentals, sustained domestic demand and favourable export performance.
Despite the encouraging outlook, Miti and Malaysia External Trade Development Corp said they will continue monitoring global trade developments closely and remain proactive in safeguarding Malaysia's trade resilience and growth momentum.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia central bank raises interest rates by more than expected
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's central bank raised its policy interest rates for the first time in two years at a policy review on Wednesday.
Bank Indonesia (BI) hiked its benchmark seven-day reverse repurchase rate by 50 basis points to 5.25 per cent.
Sixteen of 29 economists in a Reuters survey had expected a 25-basis-point increase, while the rest had expected no change.
BI also raised its overnight deposit facility and lending facility rates by the same amount, to 4.25 per cent and 6.00 per cent, respectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tourist crushed to death during elephant bathing session in India [WATCH]
Article:
KARNATAKA: A 33-year-old tourist from Chennai was killed after being caught in a fight between two elephants at the Dubare Elephant Camp in Karnataka on Sunday.
According to Berita RTM in a Facebook post, the victim, identified as Jyunesh, had visited the camp to experience elephant bathing activities and enjoy the natural surroundings.
The incident reportedly occurred during an elephant bathing session when two elephants suddenly began fighting before one of them fell and crushed the victim.
Authorities said mahouts attempted to bring the animals under control, but the situation quickly escalated, leaving the victim severely injured before she was pronounced dead.
In reactions on social media to the video originally uploaded by India First TV on Facebook, user Prasad Manyam described the incident as tragic and raised concerns over safety measures.
"So sad to see this. A man with his small child is fighting for his wife. A risk to the child and himself but yet he tried his best to rescue his wife. God bless such brave men.
"And why did the authorities allow such close proximity and are now taking action only after the loss of life. Now they may kill that elephant sadly," he wrote.
Another Facebook user, Maniwarne Marappan, urged the public to exercise caution around animals following the incident.
"Never ever get close to any animals, they can turn wild at any moment for many reasons," the user wrote.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Unity govt parties pledge support until term ends, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Component parties in the unity government have reaffirmed their commitment to support the administration until the end of its term, says Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Fahmi, who is also the government spokesman, said Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation during today's cabinet meeting for the continued backing from Barisan Nasional, Gabungan Rakyat Sabah and Gabungan Parti Sarawak.
He said the commitment was reiterated after Anwar touched on the possibility of an early general election.
"This commitment reflects the priority placed on the interests of the people and the nation.
"It also allows the government to remain fully focused on addressing economic challenges arising from the crisis in West Asia," he said at his weekly post-cabinet media briefing today.
Fahmi said the issue of the resignation of Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad was not raised during the meeting.
Instead, he said the cabinet was briefed on preparations for the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting next month, including updates that two former MPs had vacated their seats.
"No, it was not discussed," he said.
"There was only an update on preparations for the Dewan Rakyat sitting, which will begin on June 24, where two former MPs have vacated their seats. That was what was informed."
On May 17, Nik Nazmi and Rafizi announced that they were leaving PKR and taking over
Parti Bersama Malaysia
, also known as the Malaysian United Party, which was established in 2016.
On Monday, the duo said they had submitted their
resignation letters
to Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul to pursue a new political agenda and return the mandate to voters in both constituencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Brother among five charged with teen's murder
Article:
IPOH: A man and four of his friends were charged at the magistrate's court here today with the murder of his younger brother in Tanjung Rambutan last week.
S.K. Siri Sakti Vell, 22, together with K. Thirucelvan, 33, Shashipal Singh Manmuhan Singh, 36, N. Thaneshwaran, 24, and P. Ukenthiran, 35, as well as another person still at large, were accused of murdering 18-year-old S.K. Kerisnan.
The offence was allegedly committed in the vicinity of the Sri Subramaniar Temple along Jalan Perpaduan Southward, Tanjung Rambutan, between 1am and 4am on May 12.
The charge was framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of up to 40 years and, if the death sentence is not imposed, a minimum of 12 strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
No plea was recorded after the charge was read before magistrate Norizan Riduan, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
Deputy public prosecutor Zarris Sofea Zainudin appeared for the prosecution, while all the accused were unrepresented. The court fixed July 26 for further mention.
It was previously reported that a dispute over cable theft is believed to have led to the teenager's death. His body was later discovered inside a car along Jalan Lang Indah A/5 in the area last week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: British Land beats profit expectations as AI boom lifts demand for office space
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Real estate group British Land Company reported annual profit above market estimates on Wednesday, bolstered by robust demand for office spaces from a booming AI and technology industry.
Demand from AI and technology firms like Claude creator Anthropic for office space in London has helped British Land as its retail parks approach full occupancy, leading to strong rental growth.
The company stuck to its 2027 earnings forecast, even as it reported an underlying profit of £294 million (US$393.6 million) for the year ended March 31, compared with analyst expectations of £291 million, according to a company-compiled consensus.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zara inquest: Father seen only once since 2018, court told
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Mahathir Mohd Hashim was seen only once when his daughter, Zara Qairina, was at Queen Elizabeth Hospital, the court heard.
Abu Bakar Lamat, 47, testified that it was the only time he had seen his brother-in-law since 2018.
"Whenever I returned to my village, I never saw her father from 2018 until now, except when Zara Qairina was at Queen Elizabeth Hospital. That's it.
"Her relationship with her father, I do not know. Zara is happy with her mother.
"Since Zara was born until she became a teenager, she was never scolded, beaten or tortured, as she was the only child of her mother," he said, adding that he did not raise any questions about Mahathir to Zara Qairina as those were sensitive family matters.
The 72nd witness testified before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 70th day of the inquest into the death of his niece.
Abu Bakar, who is the elder brother of Zara's mother, Noraidah Lamat, said he returned to Sabah about four times a year to visit the mother and daughter in Sipitang.
The Kuala Lumpur-based retired soldier also said he had no knowledge of whether Zara Qairina had any step-siblings or what her relationship with them was.
His wife, Nur Shira Abdullah, however, testified yesterday that Zara Qairina has two step-siblings.
Conducting officer Dacia Jane Romanus also questioned Abu Bakar on how well he knew Zara Qairina, including her best friends in primary and secondary school, her diaries and the "barcode" incident.
The witness said he had no knowledge of those matters.
During earlier proceedings, a medical doctor explained that "barcode injuries" can be caused by sharp objects, with the pattern resembling a barcode.
Earlier, lawyer Shahlan Jufri played several videos and audio recordings to confirm Abu Bakar's understanding of Zara Qairina's behaviour with family members and her tone of conversation with her mother.
In the audio recordings, the 13-year-old was heard sharing that seniors were tutoring her in several subjects, discussing her expenses, asking Noraidah to guess about an illness, and questioning her mother's whereabouts, among other things.
Abu Bakar confirmed that it was normal for the mother and daughter to communicate in such a manner.
The inquest resumes at 2.15pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor Dredging set to unlock RM192mil potential from Hotel Maya lease
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Selangor Dredging Bhd (SDB) is unlocking the value of one of its prime hospitality assets, with a long-term leasing arrangement for Hotel Maya Kuala Lumpur that could generate up to RM192.15 million in recurring rental income over 20 years.
The property developer said its wholly owned unit, SDB Properties Sdn Bhd, has entered into a lease agreement with Kin Hotel KL City Centre Sdn Bhd for Hotel Maya Kuala Lumpur, located at 138 Jalan Ampang, alongside meeting and conference facilities at Plaza 138 currently used by the hotel.
The lease, effective July 1, 2026, will run for an initial 10-year period, with an option for the tenant to extend the agreement by two additional five-year terms, potentially stretching the partnership to two decades. Kin Hotel is wholly owned by Kin Living Pte Ltd.
Under the arrangement, SDB stands to secure a steady stream of rental income while reducing direct exposure to the operational complexities of running a hotel business. Monthly base rent has been set at RM750,000 for the first five years, rising to RM780,000 for the remaining five years of the initial lease term.
Should Kin Hotel exercise both renewal options, rental rates will increase further to RM830,000 per month for the first extension and RM880,000 per month for the subsequent term. Factoring in a three-month rent-free period, SDB estimates the total rental income from the initial 10-year lease at approximately RM89.55 million. Over a full 20-year tenure, the figure could climb to RM192.15 million.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, SDB said the move aligns with its strategy to enhance the income-generating potential of its investment properties while building a more stable and recurring earnings base from existing assets.
"The lease enables SDB to appoint an external hotel operator to assume the management and operation of Hotel Maya Kuala Lumpur, while preserving the group's ownership of the said premises," the company said.
The arrangement also allows SDB to reduce operational burdens linked to hospital management, including staffing, licensing, maintenance, guest services and other hospitality-related obligations, which will be undertaken entirely by Kin Hotel under the lease.
Importantly, the deal comes with a built-in asset enhancement plan. Kin Hotel is required to invest a minimum of US$3 million in renovation, upgrading and repositioning works within 36 months from the lease commencement.
The planned refurbishment is expected to include upgrades to guest rooms; the hotel lobby and arrival experience; selected public areas; back-of-house facilities; mechanical and electrical systems; as well as branding and related works.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother feared abduction as boy trapped in lift in Johor Baru flats
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Trapped in a malfunctioning lift for nearly 3 hours, a 9-year-old boy was rescued safe and unharmed from a public housing block in Kempas here on Saturday, after his mother initially feared he had been abducted.
The boy's mother, who only wished to be known as Dappa, 30, said the ordeal began about 4pm when her son went downstairs to dispose of rubbish at Block A of the Projek Perumahan Rakyat (PPR) Kempas but failed to return home.
She said panic set in after her elder sister, who lives in the same block, called to ask if the child had returned to their fourth-floor unit.
"My sister, who stays on the seventh floor, contacted me after my son did not come back upstairs. She asked whether he had returned home and we became anxious as nobody knew where he was," she said when contacted.
Dappa said she searched around the flats, contacted the boy's friends and checked the playground, but could not find him.
Fearing the worst, she then went to the Kempas police station to lodge a report.
"I thought my son had been kidnapped before residents nearby contacted us saying they heard screams for help from inside the lift," she said.
The child was eventually found about 7pm trapped inside a lift stalled on the second floor.
Dappa said her son appeared exhausted and drenched in sweat but did not suffer breathing difficulties.
"We are grateful he was found safe and thankful to the concerned residents who acted quickly. My son said he tried pressing the emergency button but it did not work and he could not force open the lift doors," she said.
She claimed the incident was not isolated, alleging several residents had previously been trapped in lifts due to recurring breakdowns.
"The situation has become worrying, especially for children and elderly residents who can easily panic if trapped. There are three lifts here but some frequently break down. We hope the authorities replace them before something more serious happens," she said.S
Dappa added that residents were disappointed no immediate response was triggered despite the lifts being monitored by closed-circuit television cameras.
"The flats management would usually monitor the CCTV and take immediate action if someone became trapped, but this time help only came after neighbours heard my son shouting," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 26 new LRT Kelana Jaya Line trains fully operational by 2031
Article:
PETALING JAYA: A fleet of 26 new trains for the LRT Kelana Jaya Line is expected to be fully operational by 2031 as part of long-term efforts to improve reliability on the ageing network.
Rapid Rail LRT chief operating officer Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof, said the first of the new trains is scheduled to arrive in 2029, with the replacement programme expected to be completed by the end of 2030.
"Insya-Allah, by 2031, we will be able to operate with the new trains," he said during a media briefing today.
Nor Azmi said while the arrival of the new trains would provide a long-term solution, Rapid Rail is taking immediate steps to keep the existing fleet running safely and reliably.
He said the operator has adopted a condition-based maintenance approach, allowing components to be replaced proactively based on their condition rather than strictly following manufacturer timelines.
"If the manual says a component should be replaced after six months or a year, we may replace it earlier if inspections show signs of wear, especially given the age of the trains," he said.
His remarks came in response to questions over recurring service disruptions on the Kelana Jaya Line, including an incident on May 12 involving a melted battery cable.
Nor Azmi said the incident was the first of its kind and had prompted a detailed inspection of all trains, with no similar warning signs detected so far.
"We take this seriously. The ageing factor of the trains is something we consider, and we have carried out comprehensive checks on all trains to ensure they are safe for operations," he said.
However, he said not all disruptions could be attributed to ageing trains, noting that some issues stemmed from temporary instability linked to ongoing system upgrades.
"Some disruptions may be perceived as system failures by passengers, but they could be related to system improvements currently being carried out. These systems require time to stabilise," he said.
Using the analogy of a "bathtub curve", Nor Azmi said newly upgraded systems often experience minor issues in their early stages before achieving stability.
"Typically, it takes between one and two years for a system to reach stable performance. That is why projects come with a two-year defect liability period, during which performance is closely monitored and improvements are made," he said.
Meanwhile, Nor Azmi acknowledged rising passenger demand, with the Kelana Jaya Line recording a load factor of 109 per cent in April this year, up sharply amid growing reliance on public transport.
He said Rapid Rail would increase the number of trains in operation where possible, while implementing crowd management measures to ensure commuter safety.
"We understand that more people are depending on public transport, especially with fuel subsidy changes and the government's push for greater public transport use.
"This is a heavy responsibility for us, but Prasarana and Rapid Rail will continue doing everything we can to provide the best possible service despite the constraints we face," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cries of relief and joy after five acquitted of drugs charges [WATCH]
Article:
KLANG: The silence inside the High Court as the judge delivered her verdict was broken by cries of relief and joy today after five men were acquitted of drug trafficking and possession charges.
Family members of labourers K. Venaigaraj, 34, S. Yogapretip, 33, M. Ganesan, 45, G. Sharathselvam, 31, and a 19-year-old, who had packed the public gallery, wept as the court ordered the men's release.
Judge Norliza Othman ordered the men's release after ruling that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case.
The five men were escorted from the dock to the lock-up area before having their handcuffs removed, allowing them to walk free from the courtroom for the first time in nearly three years.
None of the men wished to be photographed or speak to the media, leaving the court complex quietly after spending almost three years in remand at the Sungai Buloh Prison.
The teenager had been detained at the Puncak Alam Correctional Centre as he was a minor.
In her ruling, Norliza held there were significant gaps in the prosecution's case, particularly in proving the elements of possession, control and knowledge of the drugs, ultimately leading to the collapse of the charges.
She said the prosecution had failed to call three material witnesses to dispute the trio's sworn statement which indicated that no drugs were found on the accused.
"It is not appropriate for the court to call upon the accused to enter their defence merely to hear their version of events.
"I am satisfied that even if the accused were called to defend themselves and they choose to remain silent, the court would still not convict them based on the evidence presently before the court.
"There are doubts and gaps in the prosecution's case, particularly due to witnesses who were not called to testify.," she said.
Norliza said while the prosecution has the discretion to choose which witnesses to call, it had no choice but to call those witnesses where there were conflicting versions of events, to prove otherwise.
"This is especially so when the evidence of these witnesses contradicts that of the raiding officer.
"Based on these reasons, after evaluating the prosecution's evidence to the maximum, the court finds that the prosecution has failed to establish a prima facie case against all the accused on the charges.
"Therefore, the court acquits and discharges all of you(accused)  without calling for your defence," said the judge in her decision at the end of the prosecution's case.
After the case was adjourned, the men's family members rushed to the dock to hug and kiss them.
One of the accused was also seen clutching photographs of his family.
Speaking after the proceedings, defence lawyer Saranjeet Kaur Sidhu said her clients were relieved and grateful to finally be able to rebuild their lives after years in remand.
[[nid:1444075]]
The prosecution was represented by Deputy Public Prosecutor Nurul Ashiqin Zulkifli.
The prosecution's case was heard over five days beginning March 30, with eight witnesses called to testify.
On the first and second charges, they were jointly charged with trafficking 284.6 grammes of methamphetamine and 35.3 grammes of heroin and monoacetylmorphines.
They were alleged to have committed the offences at  10.40am at Batu 11, Kapar on Nov 7, 2023.
The charges were framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act punishable under Section 39B(2) read with Section 34 of the Penal Code.
It provides the death penalty or a life imprisonment. If the death penalty is not imposed, one must be whipped not less than 12 times both upon conviction.
Separately, Venaigaraj faced a charge of trafficking 68.3 grammes of  methamphetamine under 39B(1)(a) of the act punishable under Section 39B(2).
He was also charged with possessing 0.98 grammes of heroin and monoacetylmorphines under Section 12(2) of the same act punishable under Section 12(3).
It provides a maximum RM100,000 fine or  not more than five years jail or both upon conviction.
He allegedly committed the offences at 10.15am at the roadside of Taman Seri Kapar on Nov 7, 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six century-old shophouses destroyed in Segamat fire
Article:
SEGAMAT: Six century-old wooden shophouses were destroyed in a fire at Jalan Abdullah, Buloh Kasap, here this morning.
The 8.15am fire caused total destruction to the upper wooden floors of the shops, which included a fruit store, a traditional medicine shop, a fertiliser store, a rubber trading shop and a restaurant.
Ibrahim Ahmed, 51, said he had just opened his shop at 8am when a family member spotted thick smoke rising from the fruit store.
"Thank goodness someone alerted us about the fire, otherwise I do not know what would have happened to us," he said.
Ibrahim, who has rented and operated his business at the premises for 17 years, said the fire started in the storeroom of the fruit shop before spreading to adjacent units.
"I immediately ordered my workers to save the goods, but the fire spread too quickly because the upper part of the premises was made of wood. We felt the intense heat and could only save a few items. We could only watch the building burn. We were helpless," he said.
Bandar Baru Segamat Fire and Rescue station operations commander Muhd Hazim Abd Razak said they received an emergency call at 8.23am.
He said 16 firefighters and assets from the Segamat station were deployed to the scene.
"The operation team used water lines from fire engine pumps and hydrants to extinguish the flames.
"Firefighting operations are ongoing to ensure the fire is completely put out. The cause of the fire and total losses are still under investigation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia records strongest trust levels yet, employers most trusted institution
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Malaysia's overall trust level has reached an all-time high in the 2026, according to a global survey by Edelman.
But the Edelman Trust Barometer Malaysia survey showed that growing distrust among people with differing views and backgrounds could pose a challenge to sustaining the country's trust advantage.
The global survey found that Malaysia's trust index rose to 71 in 2026 from 66 last year, placing the country seventh globally among 28 markets surveyed.
The annual trust index measures public trust in four key institutions namely business, government, media and non-governmental organisations (NGOs).
According to the report, all four institutions in Malaysia remained within "trust territory", with business recording 76 per cent trust, followed by government and NGOs at 72 per cent each, while media stood at 65 per cent.
Employers recorded the highest trust levels at 82 per cent among employees surveyed, making employers the most trusted institution in Malaysia's landscape.
However, the report highlighted rising concerns over "insular trust," where Malaysians are increasingly hesitant to trust people with different values, political views, cultural backgrounds or sources of information.
On average, 65 per cent of Malaysians were found to hold an insular trust mindset.
A further 87 per cent said distrust toward people with differences had become so severe that individuals actively tried to make things worse for one another, the highest level recorded globally.
The study also found only 37 per cent of Malaysians regularly seek information from sources with differing political viewpoints, reflecting what the firm described as a growing retreat into familiar information environments.
Chief executive officer and senior advisor for Edelman Southeast Asia Mazuin Zin said Malaysia's strong institutional trust levels were a significant achievement.
But she warned the country could not sustain its trust advantage if social divisions continued to deepen.
Mazuin said trust in Malaysia was still largely built within familiar groups sharing similar values, communities and information sources.
"Bridging them is not just a social imperative; it is the precondition for the next phase of our growth, and communications play an important role in getting everyone in line," she added.
Globally, the survey found developing markets such as China, India, Indonesia and the United Arab Emirates remained largely in "trust-positive" territory. Developed economies including Germany, Japan, France and the United Kingdom continued to struggle with distrust.
Despite Malaysia's strong headline trust score, Edelman said the aggregate figure masked deeper structural concerns, particularly widening social and economic divisions.
The report highlighted growing economic anxiety globally and locally.
Globally, 67 per cent of employees worry about losing their jobs due to a looming recession, the highest level recorded to date.
Meanwhile, 66 per cent of employees worldwide are concerned international trade conflicts and tariffs could negatively affect their employers, also an all-time high and up eight percentage points since 2019.
Edelman said such concerns were particularly relevant for Malaysia as one of the world's most trade-dependent economies.
"Fears surrounding global supply chain disruptions, tariffs and geopolitical tensions were increasingly shaping how Malaysians viewed economic stability and future opportunities," it said.
In Malaysia, only 39 per cent of respondents believe the next generation will be better off than today, down seven percentage points from 2025.
The decline in optimism reflected concerns over rising living costs, economic uncertainty and broader global instability.
It also highlighted widening income-based trust gaps.
"The income-based trust gap for the trust index in the Asia Pacific region stands at 16 points between high and low-income groups, a figure that has more than doubled since 2012.
"In Malaysia specifically, a nine-point trust gap persists between the high and low-income groups," the report said.
The study found lower-income groups generally showed lower trust levels toward institutions and required greater consistency, transparency and accountability from leaders to maintain confidence.
At the same time, concerns over disinformation continued to intensify, with 73 per cent of Malaysians worried that foreign actors were deliberately spreading falsehoods through local media to inflame domestic divisions.
This was the second-highest figure globally after the United Arab Emirates.
The survey also identified a widening "domestic trust premium" in Malaysia, where locally headquartered companies were trusted significantly more than foreign firms.
Malaysian companies recorded 77 per cent trust compared with 59 per cent for foreign companies, creating an 18-point trust gap.
Nearly half of Malaysians said they would put less effort into helping a colleague or manager whose political beliefs differed from their own, while 38 per cent said they would rather switch departments than work under a manager with different values.
Another 43 per cent said they would support reducing the number of foreign companies operating in Malaysia, even if it resulted in higher prices.
The report warned that insularity was no longer merely a social issue but an economic challenge affecting workplace collaboration, productivity and talent mobility.
In the Asia Pacific region, 77 per cent of employees said employers had an obligation to help build trust between groups that distrust one another, although only 54 per cent believed employers were doing this effectively.
This resulted in a 23-point expectation and performance gap. The gaps were even wider for government and media at 37 points each, and NGOs at 31 points.
The survey found 38 per cent of Malaysians believe businesses build trust most effectively during divisive social issues by encouraging cooperation without taking sides.
This was higher than those who preferred businesses to take a values-based position at 32 per cent, while 11 per cent believed businesses should remain silent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Love and war: Supreme leader urges Iran to procreate
Article:
PARIS: In a break from his recent warlike rhetoric, the latest message from Iran's shadowy supreme leader on Tuesday set his people a more productive mission: get out and make babies.
Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei was reportedly wounded in the US-Israeli bombardment that killed his father and predecessor and ignited the Middle East war on Feb 28, and he has not been seen in public since.
But several written messages have been attributed to him and in the latest – a letter in response to well-wishes from pronatalists – he urged Iranians to secure great power status by growing their population.
"By earnestly pursuing the correct, necessary policy of population growth, the great Iranian nation will be able to play a major role and experience strategic leaps in the future," he posted on social media platform X.
In a longer version of his remarks carried by state broadcaster IRIB, Khamenei told activists: "It is hoped that your sincere efforts... will lead to fruitful results, God willing."
Iran has a population of around 92 million – less than half that of neighbouring Pakistan and smaller than Middle East rival Egypt – but official figures show it is still the 17th most populous nation in the world.
The country's fertility rate has however fallen drastically in recent decades, from 6.5 in 1979, the year of the Islamic revolution, to just 1.7 in 2024, according to World Bank figures.
In 2020, an Iranian health official said state hospitals and clinics had stopped providing vasectomies or handing out contraceptives in order to turbo-charge population growth.
Despite the damage inflicted by recent US and Israeli air and missile strikes – and the death of Khamenei senior and several senior figures – Teheran believes it is emerging stronger from the conflict.
Teheran has exchanged proposals for peace negotiations with Washington, but is still demanding sanctions relief and insists on both maintaining control of the Strait of Hormuz and retaining the right to enrich nuclear fuel.
To maintain this regional leadership role and take "long strides toward building the new Islamic-Iranian civilisation", Khamenei said, Iranians must promote a "culture of childbearing."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Engine oil issue forces RMAF Hornet to land at KKIA
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An engine oil system emergency forced a Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) F/A-18D Hornet fighter jet to make an emergency landing at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) last night.
RMAF chief General Datuk Seri Muhamad Norazlan Aris said the incident occurred during a routine flight, prompting emergency procedures to be activated.
"The aircraft was on a routine flight. There was an emergency involving the engine oil system. The engine was shut down and the aircraft landed safely," he told the New Straits Times.
He said no injuries were reported in the incident and further inspections are being conducted on the aircraft.
It was reported that the
aircraft had experienced a technical issue
involving its left engine before landing safely at 7.47pm.
The fighter jet was later parked at Terminal 2 at KKIA for further action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three former TM USA executives no longer with company, says TM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three former employees of TM USA Inc implicated in a United States Department of Justice (DOJ) probe are no longer with Telekom Malaysia Bhd (TM).
TM said it had launched an internal investigation into the individuals before any legal action by United States authorities, which ultimately led to their dismissal.
TM said it was aware of recent reports concerning the three individuals and stressed that it was proactively addressing the matter.
"The company had earlier initiated its own investigation following suspicions of misconduct involving the individuals, which led to their dismissal," the company said in a statement.
It was reported earlier that the United States had charged three senior TM employees for allegedly misappropriating more than US$20 million from the Malaysian telecommunications company, the DOJ said on Tuesday.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1443955/us-charges-three-malaysian-state-telecoms-officials-multimillion-dollar
Mohd Hafiz Lockman, Mohd Yuzaimi Yusof and Khanh Thuong Nguyen, who were senior executives at TM's United States subsidiary, were accused of using false statements and forged records to siphon funds from the company and deceive counterparties, suppliers, auditors and supervisors in the United States on various occasions between July 2020 and February 2026, authorities said.
According to TM, the findings and information gathered from its internal investigation were subsequently shared with the relevant authorities.
"The matter is currently under the purview of the US DOJ and the US courts. The company will continue to extend its full cooperation to the DOJ."
TM reiterated its stance on corporate integrity, stressing that it upholds strict governance and compliance standards and maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards any form of misconduct.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelana Jaya LRT passenger numbers nearly double to 300,000 daily
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The number of Light Rail Transit (LRT) passengers on the Kelana Jaya Line has nearly doubled since 2020, with an uptick observed since the start of the Middle East conflict, according to operator Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd.
Rapid Rail LRT chief operating officer Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof said weekday ridership in April averaged 289,282 passengers a day, up from 150,889 in 2020, a 109 per cent increase over six years.
"The figures represent an average annual growth rate of about 11.5 per cent, with the upward trend expected to continue in the coming years," he said during a briefing on Kelana Jaya Line operations and upgrade works.
Nor Azmi said the increase was observed during the Iran-US conflict, rising fuel prices and reductions in subsidised fuel quotas.
"However, there was a slight dip when the government announced work-from-home measures.
"Overall, we have seen many users shifting from private vehicles to public transport, which is a positive development," he said.
He said Rapid Rail would continue enhancing its services to accommodate growing demand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nga expands 'Simen Rahmah' scheme, cuts cement prices by up to 32pct
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has announced an expansion of the 'Simen Rahmah' initiative, cutting cement prices by up to 32 per cent to help keep housing affordable.
"Our mission is clear: people's housing must remain affordable. This synergy between the public and private sectors is a concrete step to ensure the cost of living remains manageable despite global economic uncertainties," said Nga at a press conference on the Simen Murah scheme.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Capital A's growth prospects stay bright despite Iran war, says HLIB
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Capital A Bhd's expansion plans and earnings growth prospects remain bright despite the ongoing Iran war.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said following its first quarter (Q1) financial year 2026 (FY26) core earnings of RM24.8 million, Capital A has now achieved two consecutive profitable quarters post-restructuring.
"Following two consecutive quarters of profits, Capital A's PN17 status will be uplifted effective May 20. Capital A is still confident of its internal target of RM3.8 billion in revenue and RM266 million in net operating profit, riding on a seasonally stronger second half (2H)," it said.
Meanwhile, HLIB said Asia Digital Engineering (ADE), the maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) arm of Capital A Bhd, continues to deliver strong performance, supported by resilient air travel demand.
It noted that the company's existing 16 hangars are already fully booked for 2026, while construction of an additional four hangars is targeted for completion by end-2027.
Capital A is also exploring regional merger and acquisition (M&A) opportunities (targeting 20 hangars by 2029).
"In addition, ADE is securing more line maintenance contracts from third-party airlines while expanding into ASEAN and Bahrain (despite the ongoing Iran war)," it said.
In addition, HLIB said Capital A's logistics arm, Teleport Everywhere Pte Ltd, continues to record strong volume growth, benefiting from the ongoing e-commerce boom, particularly as China expands its global trade footprint.
Looking ahead, it said Teleport is expected to deliver seasonally stronger volumes and improved margins in 2HFY26.
Overall, HLIB has reiterated its Buy recommendation on Capital A, with an unchanged target price of 76 sen.
"We remain optimistic about the company's expansion plans and leverage Air Asia X Bhd's growth over the longer term. Upliftment of its PN17 status should boost investors' confidence and support valuation re-rating," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SC tightens digital asset rules to enhance investor protection
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission Malaysia (SC) has enhanced the guidelines on recognised markets, marking a step in the evolution of the regulatory framework for Digital Asset Exchange (DAX).
The revised guidelines are intended to accelerate product rollouts on regulated DAX platforms, strengthen investor protection, and improve the operational resilience of these platforms.
The move will strengthen the competitiveness of regulated DAX operators while reinforcing investor protection and improving the overall resilience of Malaysia's digital asset segment.
"Furthermore, the SC is tightening regulatory action against unregulated digital asset activities with administrative action taken against four DAXs for operating without registration.
"Meanwhile, the SC has also worked with technology companies such as Google to limit unregistered DAX from promoting their services to Malaysians via social media platforms and channels, starting April 14, 2026," it said in a statement.
This aligns Malaysia's digital asset landscape with global developments, amid increasing institutional participation in the asset class.
The SC said Malaysia's digital asset market continues to record steady growth, with total trading value on regulated digital asset exchanges rising to RM17.14 billion in 2025, a 23 per cent increase from RM13.93 billion in 2024.
It added that the enhanced framework aligns with the Capital Market Masterplan 2026–2030, which aims to expand Malaysia's capital market to between RM5.8 trillion and RM6.3 trillion by 2030.
SC chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said the digital asset industry must continue to evolve and adopt the institutional discipline expected of a more mature capital market.
"Our enhanced guideline demands resilient and credible partners within Malaysia's financial market ecosystem. As the market grows more inclusive and innovative, it must be balanced with the highest standards of governance," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Royal institution serves as anchor for national unity, constitutional supremacy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The royal institution holds a high position and carries the responsibility of providing views and advice to ensure the continuity of the country's system of governance is always preserved.
Selangor Royal Council member Datuk Salehuddin Saidin said although Malaysia practises a parliamentary democracy system, the royal institution still plays an important role as provided under the Federal Constitution.
"The role of the royal institution is not limited to appointing the head of government, dissolving the Dewan Rakyat and State Legislative Assemblies, but also includes advising the government and acting on the government's advice in matters involving national administration.
"In this context, the royal institution serves as a check-and-balance element to ensure the government's administration remains centred on the interests of the people, national stability and the supremacy of the Constitution," he told BH.
Salehuddin said the principles of the Rukun Negara, particularly the supremacy of the Constitution and the rule of law, must be understood comprehensively to ensure national unity continues to be safeguarded.
He said the principle of the supremacy of the Constitution means that the people must accept and uphold the Constitution as the supreme law of the land, which forms the foundation of stability and harmony in a multiracial society.
"The principle of the rule of law also requires the people to respect and comply with all laws enacted to preserve peace and national unity.
"This includes compliance with laws related to sensitive issues involving race, religion and royalty, or 3R, which were introduced to prevent tension within society.
"If there are parties who violate laws related to sensitive issues, action can be taken because such laws were enacted to ensure national unity remains protected," he said.
Salehuddin also said that the principle of courtesy and morality requires the people, political leaders and elected representatives to maintain decorum when expressing views or criticism, especially towards the royal institution and national leadership.
"Loyalty to the King and country is not merely symbolic, but must be translated through respect for national institutions, adherence to the law and the use of courteous language in public and digital spaces," he said.
Commenting on the lack of understanding of the Rukun Negara, Salehuddin said education on the matter should be strengthened at all levels of schooling to ensure the people better understand the nation's history, system of governance and the importance of national unity.
He said the lack of understanding stemmed from insufficient in-depth exposure to the country's history, the social contract and the role of the royal institution in Malaysia's system of governance.
"The spirit of patriotism cannot be separated from understanding the Constitutional Monarchy and Parliamentary Democracy system practised since independence.
"When we speak about loyalty to the country, the people must understand the history behind the formation of Malaysia, the position of its multiracial society and how the Federal Constitution was formed through inter-ethnic consensus," he said.
He added that the nation-building process involved consensus among the Malay, Chinese and Indian communities, which was later translated into the Federal Constitution, including provisions related to Malay special privileges, the Malay language and Islam.
He said the racial riots in 1969 also became an important turning point in the formation of the Rukun Negara as the foundation of national unity and a guide for Malaysians in preserving harmony in a multiracial society.
"The five principles of the Rukun Negara comprise belief in God, loyalty to King and country, supremacy of the Constitution, rule of law, and courtesy and morality. All these aim to ensure national integration continues to be preserved," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect held over alleged attack attempt with parang at mosque in Batu Pahat
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A man believed to be under the influence of drugs has been remanded for 3 days after allegedly attempting to attack a speaker with a parang during a lecture at a mosque in Simpang Tiga, Parit Sulong, here, on Monday.
The remand order against the 38-year-old suspect was granted by magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman, effective from today until Friday.
This allows police to complete investigations under Section 506 of the Penal Code, including recording the suspect's statement, conducting a verification parade and carrying out further probes.
Section 506 covers criminal intimidation involving threats to cause alarm, death or grievous hurt.
If found guilty, the offence carries a penalty of up to 7 years' imprisonment, or a fine, or both.
According to a police report, the incident occurred at 8.20pm while the speaker, in his 40s, was delivering a lecture to the congregation.
The suspect, who was attending the session, allegedly stood up in anger while holding a parang.
He then became aggressive and attempted to attack the speaker with the weapon.
Members of the congregation intervened and restrained him.
The suspect later fled the mosque. Acting on a report, a police team from the Gambling, Vice and Secret Societies Division (D7) arrested him yesterday.
Initial investigations found the suspect claimed he acted after overhearing the speaker allegedly insulting the Prophet during the lecture.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China to work with US on slashing tariffs
Article:
BEIJING: Beijing will work with Washington on reducing levies affecting tens of billions of dollars in goods, a commerce ministry statement said Wednesday, days after US President Donald Trump visited China.
The world's two top economies spent much of 2025 embroiled in an escalating trade war, until the two leaders reached a one-year truce when they met in South Korea in October.
As a result of their summit last week, a trade council has been set up, under the auspices of which "both sides agreed in principle to discuss a framework arrangement for reciprocal tariff reductions on products of equivalent scale", the ministry said Wednesday.
The intended tariff cuts will affect goods worth "$30 billion or more on each side", the online statement, attributed to an unnamed commerce ministry official, said.
China hopes "the US side will honour its commitment" made during the recent round of negotiations, it added, calling for an extension to the trade truce agreements reached last year.
The commerce ministry also said that China would restore registrations for some US beef exporters, following their lapse last year during the height of tensions with Washington.
Confirming another outcome of the Xi-Trump summit, the ministry said China will purchase 200 aircraft from US aerospace giant Boeing, though it did not specify which model or models.
US media had reported for several months that Beijing was poised to make a major order from Boeing that would include 500 single-aisle 737 MAXs and about 100 larger 787 Dreamliners and 777s.
On the supply of rare earths – the critical field dominated by China and the target of biting export restrictions implemented last year – the statement was scant in detail.
"Both sides will work together to study and resolve each other's legitimate and lawful concerns," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bina Darulaman unit bags RM251.65mil Kedah road maintenance job
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bina Darulaman Bhd's (BDB) subsidiary BDB Infra Sdn Bhd has secured a RM251.65 million state road maintenance contract from the Kedah state government.
The 60-month (five-year) contract involves road maintenance works in Zone 1, covering the districts of Kota Setar, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap and Pokok Sena.
BDB group chief executive officer Che Abdul Khalid Md Din said the award reflects the
The Kedah state government's continued confidence in BDB Infra's capability and credibility in delivering infrastructure projects efficiently, consistently and with high-quality standards.
"This contract award is a meaningful recognition for BDB and BDB Infra, reflecting our extensive experience and proven track record in the road maintenance and construction sector.
"BDB remains committed to ensuring the project is implemented in accordance with the required specifications and standards to enhance the safety and comfort of road users in Kedah," he said in a statement today.
He said the contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings and financial performance from 2026 to 2031 while boosting confidence in its long-term growth prospects.
According to him, the project is also expected to generate positive spillover effects on the
state's infrastructure development and stimulate local economic activities through employment opportunities and participation from local supply chain players.
BDB Infra is a Grade G7 contractor with more than 50 years of experience in road maintenance and construction works, as well as quarry operations in the northern region of Peninsular Malaysia.
BDB Infra has continuously secured road maintenance contracts from the Kedah state government since 2015, covering several districts statewide.
Over a 10-year period since 2015, BDB Infra has continuously received road maintenance contracts from the Kedah state government, covering several districts across the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SNCC: No confirmation on GSF participants' whereabouts [WATCH]
Article:
SEPANG: There is still no confirmation on whether participants of the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0, including Malaysians detained by Israeli forces yesterday, have arrived at Ashdod Port.
Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said lawyers from the Centre for Human Rights Research and Advocacy (Centhra) and the Legal Centre for Arab Minority Rights in Israel (Adalah) are currently on the ground monitoring the situation.
"So far, information obtained by Centhra through the Adalah team on the ground, particularly at Ashdod Port, has yet to confirm the presence of all participants," he said during a press conference today.
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Sumud Nusantara (@sumudnusantaraofficial)
He said the lawyers are also on standby in Greece, Jordan and Egypt to receive the participants upon their release.
He added that Israeli forces could be delaying the transfer of the detainees as a tactic to slow down the release process.
"Once they arrive on land, Israel must comply with its own laws, which require detainees to be released within 24 hours," Sani said.
"Currently, the activists are facing detention and possibly aggressive treatment. Our responsibility on the ground is to continue every effort within our means.
"If there is still no information regarding their arrival at Ashdod Port within 48 hours, I believe it would constitute a serious incident requiring international intervention."
Sani said the mission complied with all maritime standards as a non-violent humanitarian effort.
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Sumud Nusantara (@sumudnusantaraofficial)
"Once again, it is clear that international law has been disregarded and violated. The world cannot continue to remain silent and merely observe.
"Although Malaysia does not have diplomatic relations with Israel, Malaysia's voice continues to be heard at the international level. Now is the time to work together with all relevant parties to intensify pressure and efforts, not only to secure their release, but also to ensure justice is upheld."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi says Putrajaya’s expenditure realignment exercise reaches 50pc of target
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said the government’s expenditure realignment exercise has achieved about 50 per cent of its target so far.
He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim informed the Cabinet that measures implemented across ministries based on current priorities had begun to show positive results.
“The prime minister also informed the Cabinet that expenditure realignment measures implemented across ministries based on current priorities had begun to show positive results without affecting service delivery or the implementation of ministry programmes.
“Priority continues to be given to strengthening frontline services, particularly in the healthcare, security and education sectors, to ensure the welfare of the people continues to be safeguarded,” he told a weekly press conference at the ministry here today.
Asked whether there was a detailed breakdown of savings achieved by each ministry under the exercise, Fahmi said no specific figures had been provided.
Separately, Fahmi said the Cabinet welcomed an initiative by the Federation of Malaysian Manufacturers (FMM) to conduct a survey aimed at identifying sector-specific challenges faced by industries and businesses among its members.
“The findings of the survey can help the government formulate and coordinate initiatives in a more strategic, focused and effective manner to ensure industry-related issues can be addressed comprehensively,” he said.

Title: BN, GRS and GPS reaffirm support for unity government until end of term, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had informed the Cabinet that Barisan Nasional (BN), Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) had reaffirmed their support for the unity government and pledged to remain with the administration until the end of the current term.
Fahmi said the prime minister also expressed appreciation during today’s Cabinet meeting for the continued commitment shown by component parties in the Madani government.
“He said the parties had reaffirmed their support for the unity government and their commitment to remain with the administration until the end of the current term.
“The commitment reflects the priority placed on the interests of the people and the country,” he told a weekly press conference at the ministry here.
Fahmi said the commitment would allow the government to remain focused on addressing economic challenges arising from the crisis in West Asia while ensuring the welfare of the rakyat continued to be protected.
Separately, he said no discussions were held during the Cabinet meeting regarding any resignation involving the government.
“The only matter mentioned was preparations for the Dewan Rakyat sitting beginning on June 22, including the two ministers vacating their seats,” he said.
Fahmi was referring to former ministers Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, who had officially announced their departure from PKR to join Parti Bersama Malaysia.

Title: Sultan Ibrahim confers state honours to Johor Regent’s wife and Raja Muda
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 20 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today conferred the ‘Darjah Sultan Ibrahim Johor Yang Amat Disanjungi Pangkat Pertama Dato’ Sri Mulia Sultan Ibrahim Johor (SMIJ)’ state title upon Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor, wife of Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.
According to a post on the official Facebook page of HRH Crown Prince of Johor, His Majesty also conferred the ‘Darjah Kerabat Johor Yang Amat Dihormati Pangkat Pertama (DK I)’ upon the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail.
The investiture ceremony was officiated by Tunku Ismail at Istana Besar here.
Also in attendance were Tunku Temenggong of Johor, Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Sultan Ibrahim, and Tunku Abu Bakar Ibrahim Tunku Ismail. — Bernama

Title: Brother among five charged over teen’s murder after alleged cable theft dispute
Article:
IPOH, May 20 — A man and four of his friends were charged with murdering his younger brother in Tanjung Rambutan last week, at the Magistrate’s Court here today.
S.K. Siri Sakti Vell, 22, and his friends K. Thirucelvan, 33, Shashipal Singh Manmuhan Singh, 36, N. Thaneshwaran, 24, and P. Ukenthiran, 35, together with one other individual who remains at large, are accused of murdering S.K. Kerisnan, 18.
They allegedly committed the act in the vicinity of the Sri Subramaniar Temple, Jalan Perpaduan Southward, Tanjung Rambutan, between 1 am and 4 am on May 12.
The charge was filed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which carries the death penalty or up to 40 years’ imprisonment, and if not sentenced to death, shall also be liable to no fewer than 12 strokes of the cane, upon conviction.
No plea was recorded from any of the men after the charge was read before Magistrate Norizan Riduan, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
Deputy public prosecutor Zarris Sofea Zainudin appeared for the prosecution, while all the accused were unrepresented. The court set July 26 for further case mention.
The media previously reported that a dispute over cable theft is believed to have led to the killing of a teenage boy, whose body was later found inside a car on Jalan Lang Indah A/5 in the area last week. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia still has no info on Global Sumud Flotilla activists 48 hours after Israeli abduction
Article:
SEPANG, May 20 — The Sumud Nusantara Control Centre (SNCC) confirmed it has yet to receive any updates on participants of the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 detained by Israeli forces on Monday and Tuesday.
SNCC director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim said the organisation remained in the dark over the fate of the humanitarian activists nearly 48 hours after their detention.
“We have not received any official information on their condition.
“They could have been assaulted or subjected to aggressive treatment. If there is still no official confirmation within 48 hours of their arrival at Ashdod Port, this is an incident that the world must pay attention to and intervene in,” he told a press conference on the GSF 2.0 mission here today.
Earlier reports said the mission had entered its final phase after 54 vessels departed from Marmaris Port, Turkiye, for Gaza. SNCC had also updated the yellow and red zone boundaries based on the latest security risk assessment in the operational area.
Sani Araby said participants were believed to have been placed in a “prison ship” before being taken to Ashdod Port, although official information remained limited.
“As practised last year, participants were instructed under the standard operating procedure (SOP) to dispose of their mobile phones during critical moments to avoid confiscation. Once they were abducted, all communication with the Malaysian and international delegations was completely cut off,” he said.
He urged the international community, including human rights organisations and the United Nations (UN), to pressure Israel to provide information and guarantee the activists’ safety.
“We demand transparency and safety guarantees for all humanitarian activists involved in this mission. They are there on humanitarian grounds, not as a military threat,” he said.
Sani Araby said the incident occurred as the International Criminal Court (ICC) was issuing arrest warrants against five senior Israeli forces leaders over alleged war crimes.
“This shows our delegation is now under the control of individuals accused of war crimes and wanted internationally,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Sultan Ibrahim pilots speedboat, dives underwater to inspect marine conditions in Mersing
Article:
MERSING, May 20 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today personally piloted a speedboat to inspect the waters around Mersing, reflecting His Majesty’s continued concern for marine conservation and environmental sustainability.
According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, His Majesty, who places strong emphasis on beach cleanliness and the preservation of marine ecosystems, also dived underwater during the visit to observe conditions beneath the surface.
Earlier in the morning, Sultan Ibrahim stopped for breakfast at the Midway Rest and Recreation (R&R) area in Dusun Panti, Kota Tinggi, where His Majesty enjoyed ‘lontong’.
The King’s presence pleasantly surprised members of the public who were having breakfast at the R&R stop, with many taking the opportunity to greet and take photographs with His Majesty. — Bernama

Title: Don’t get stuck: The dates Plus expects the worst traffic for the Wesak, King’s Birthday holiday bonanza
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Plus Malaysia Berhad (Plus) expects more than 2.2 million vehicles on its highway network daily during the back-to-back holidays beginning this Friday.
For the East Coast Expressway Phase 2 (LPT2), traffic flow is expected to reach up to 50,000 vehicles per day during peak periods.
In a statement today, Plus said the surge in traffic is due to the convergence of the mid-year school holidays with three major celebrations: Hari Raya Aidiladha, Wesak Day and the Official Birthday of His Majesty the King.
Peak travel days for vehicles leaving the Klang Valley are expected to fall on May 22, 23, 26, 28, 29, 30 and 31. For return journeys to the Klang Valley, peak days are forecast for June 4, 5, 6 and 7.
"Highway users are advised to plan their balik kampung or holiday trips earlier to avoid congestion. The public is also encouraged to refer to and obtain the MyPlus-TTA digital travel schedule via the Plus app," the statement read.
To ensure optimal safety and comfort, Plus will activate Smart Lanes at 33 traffic-prone locations to boost lane capacity, while deploying over 500 PlusRonda and LPT2Ronda personnel for 24-hour patrols.
Plus is also optimising operations by opening all toll plaza lanes and monitoring real-time traffic through its Traffic Monitoring Centre.
Through strategic collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police, Road Transport Department, Malaysian Highway Authority and other agencies, Plus is committed to easing traffic flow and maintaining peak safety levels.
Motorists can get live traffic updates via the Plus app, PUTRI virtual assistant, X accounts @Plustrafik and @lpt2trafik, Variable Message Signs (VMS), and major national radio stations.
"Plus advises highway users to use the Plus app, Waze or Google Maps to plan trips and check real-time traffic throughout the festive season," the statement read.
Highway users are reminded to ensure sufficient Touch 'n Go or eWallet balance (for RFID and JustGO users), keep vehicles in good condition, drive carefully, rest adequately and follow traffic rules for everyone's safety.
For emergency assistance, call PlusLine at 1800-88-0000 or LPT2Line at 1800-88-0220. — Bernama

Title: Rapid Rail says Kelana Jaya Line latest disruptions linked to upgrade instability, ageing trains
Article:
PETALING JAYA, May 20 — Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd, operator of the Kelana Jaya Line, today acknowledged shortcomings in its service following two train disruption incidents this month.
Rapid Rail LRT chief operating officer Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof said disruptions that commuters may perceive as system failures or ageing assets were actually caused by instability during ongoing system upgrades.
He said the upgrades were part of long-term efforts to ensure safer, more stable and more reliable services for commuters.
“I do not deny the fact that older trains may contribute to failures.
“When a system is being upgraded, it takes time to achieve full stability and our contractors also have to deal with operational challenges during the process.
“So, not all incidents or disruptions are caused solely by the trains’ age, but ageing is indeed a factor, I do not deny that,” he told a media briefing at the Kompleks Rapid Rail Subang here.
The Kelana Jaya Line experienced service disruptions during peak hours on May 12 and May 18 following component failures.
Nor Azmi said the May 12 incident was the first of its kind since the line began operations in 1998.
Apart from replacing 26 train sets by 2030, Rapid Rail is also carrying out upgrades involving critical systems such as radio communications and signalling.
Nor Azmi explained that upgraded systems usually require one to two years to achieve full stability after implementation.
“If we look at system upgrade instability, at the beginning, we will naturally see several issues before things stabilise.
“Once stable, the system may then operate for another five or 10 years before major failures occur again.
“Usually, achieving stable performance takes between one to two years,” he said, adding that system stability is closely monitored and improvements are continuously made during the defect liability period.
On maintenance efforts, Nor Azmi said Rapid Rail has adopted condition-based maintenance to replace components before breakdowns occur.
“With condition-based maintenance, inspections are carried out more frequently rather than following manuals which may schedule checks every six months to a year.
“For example, if within three months we notice signs of wear in any component, it will be replaced immediately and I believe this approach helps us sustain the existing trains while awaiting the arrival of new ones,” he said.
He also acknowledged passenger overcrowding as an ongoing challenge, noting that current load factors have exceeded normal levels.
Nor Azmi said average weekday ridership had risen to 289,282 passengers a day from 150,889 in 2020, representing an average annual increase of 11.5 per cent.
“To manage this load factor, we need to increase the number of trains in operation and we are also implementing crowd management measures.
“We will ensure that stations do not become overcrowded to the point of endangering passenger safety, so these are among the steps we are taking,” he said.
Nor Azmi added that Rapid Rail has taken note of public feedback and welcomed suggestions to improve services amid rising ridership driven by efforts to encourage public transport use and fuel subsidy rationalisation.
“We acknowledge this, and we also recognise that this has become a major responsibility for us.
“We hope users can also understand the situation faced by Rapid Rail because what we are doing is our very best,” he said.

Title: Johor BN says it will contest state polls solo despite pressure, ‘traitor’ labels
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 20 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) remains committed to contesting independently in the upcoming state election despite being threatened, challenged and labelled traitors, the coalition’s state information chief Md Israk Abdullah said today.
He said BN and Umno had no reason to back down from the decision and would continue to field candidates in all 56 state constituencies.
“This is because Johor has its own political mould and is not dependent on any power-sharing arrangements.
“In Johor, there is no state unity government and there is no need to create such an arrangement as the existing government has a strong mandate from the people,” he said in a statement.
